back_translation: In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah, Hoshea son of Elah became king over Israel in Samaria, and he reigned nine years.
He did evil in the eyes of the LORD, but not like the kings of Israel before him.
Shalmaneser king of Assyria came up against him, and Hoshea became his vassal and paid him tribute.

But the king of Assyria found that Hoshea had conspired, sending envoys to So king of Egypt, and not bringing tribute to the king of Assyria year by year; so the king of Assyria seized him and bound him in prison.
Then the king of Assyria invaded the whole land, went up to Samaria, and besieged it three years.
In the ninth year of Hoshea, the king of Assyria captured Samaria and carried Israel away to Assyria; he settled them in Halah, on the Habor, the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.
All this happened because the Israelites had sinned against the LORD their God, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and they feared other gods.
They walked in the customs of the nations the LORD had driven out before the Israelites, and in the customs the kings of Israel had introduced.
The Israelites secretly did things against the LORD their God that were not right; they built high places in all their towns, from watchtower to fortified city.
They set up sacred pillars and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
They burned incense on all the high places like the nations the LORD had driven out before them, and did evil things to provoke the LORD to anger.
They served idols, although the LORD had said to them, “You shall not do this thing.”

Yet the LORD warned Israel and Judah through all His prophets and seers, saying, “Turn from your evil ways and keep My commandments and statutes, according to all the law I commanded your fathers and sent to you through My servants the prophets.”
But they would not listen; they stiffened their necks like their fathers, who did not trust in the LORD their God.

They rejected His statutes and the covenant He made with their fathers, and the warnings He gave them; they followed worthless idols and became worthless, and followed the nations around them that the LORD had commanded them not to imitate.
They abandoned all the commandments of the LORD their God, made for themselves two cast calves, made an Asherah pole, bowed to all the host of heaven, and served Baal.
They sacrificed their sons and daughters in the fire, practiced divination and soothsaying, and sold themselves to do evil in the eyes of the LORD, provoking Him to anger.
So the LORD was very angry with Israel and removed them from His presence; only the tribe of Judah was left.
Even Judah did not keep the commandments of the LORD their God, but walked in the customs Israel had introduced.
So the LORD rejected all the descendants of Israel; He afflicted them and gave them into the hand of plunderers, until He had cast them from His presence.
When the LORD tore Israel away from the house of David, they made Jeroboam son of Nebat king; and Jeroboam drove Israel away from following the LORD and made them commit a great sin.
The Israelites persisted in all the sins Jeroboam committed; they did not turn from them,
until the LORD removed Israel from His presence, as He had said through all His servants the prophets. So Israel was carried away from their own land to Assyria, where they are to this day.

The king of Assyria brought people from Babylon, Cuthah, Avva, Hamath, and Sepharvaim, and settled them in the cities of Samaria in place of the Israelites; they took possession of Samaria and lived in its cities.
When they first lived there, they did not fear the LORD, so He sent lions among them, which killed some of them.

So they said to the king of Assyria, “The nations you have removed and placed in the cities of Samaria do not know the custom of the God of the land; so He has sent lions among them, killing them, because they do not know the custom of the God of the land.”
Then the king of Assyria commanded, “Send back one of the priests you carried away from there; let him go and live there and teach them the custom of the God of the land.”
So one of the priests they had carried away from Samaria came and lived in Bethel, and began to teach them how to fear the LORD.
Nevertheless, each nation made its own gods in the cities where they lived, and set them up in the shrines on the high places the Samaritans had made.
The men of Babylon made Succoth-benoth, the men of Cuth made Nergal, the men of Hamath made Ashima,
the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak, and the Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech, the gods of Sepharvaim.
They feared the LORD, but also appointed for themselves priests of the high places from all sorts of people, to offer sacrifices for them in the shrines of the high places.
They feared the LORD, but also served their own gods according to the customs of the nations from which they had been carried away.

To this day they continue their former customs; none of them truly fears the LORD or follows the statutes, ordinances, law, and commandments the LORD commanded the descendants of Jacob, whom He named Israel.
For the LORD made a covenant with the Israelites and commanded them, “Do not fear other gods, do not bow to them, do not serve them, and do not sacrifice to them.
But fear the LORD, who brought you out of the land of Egypt with great power and an outstretched arm; bow to Him and sacrifice to Him.
Be careful always to keep the statutes, ordinances, law, and commandments He wrote for you; do not fear other gods.
Do not forget the covenant I made with you, and do not fear other gods.
But fear the LORD your God, and He will deliver you from the hand of all your enemies.”
But they would not listen; they continued their former customs.
So these nations feared the LORD but also served their idols; their children and grandchildren do as their fathers did, to this day.
